title: Velvety Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: Indulge in a buttery, creamy lamb tagine with a subtle cacao twist-perfect for midnight cravings and keto-friendly dining.

ingredients.name: Unsweetened cocoa powder
ingredients.notes: Adds velvety depth and keeps carbs low

ingredients.name: Butter, unsalted
ingredients.notes: Used for searing and enriching the sauce

instructions: Pat the lamb dry and coat all sides with cumin, coriander, cinnamon, salt, and pepper.
Heat the pressure cooker on sauté mode and add butter; sear the lamb on all sides until browned, about 3–4 min per side. Remove and set aside.
Add the diced onion to the cooker and sauté until translucent, about 3 min.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 sec.
Add the cocoa powder, stirring to toast and release its aroma, about 1 min.
Pour in the beef broth, scraping the bottom to loosen browned bits.
Return the lamb to the pot, spooning some sauce over it.
Seal the pressure cooker and cook on high pressure for 35 min.
Quick‑release the pressure when time is up, then open the cooker carefully.
Use an immersion blender to blend the sauce until silky and velvety—stop before it becomes too smooth to keep a hint of texture.
Serve the tagine hot, optionally garnishing with fresh herbs if desired.
